provenance.role: The machine-readable per-harness enforcement matrix (P20 / M10 standing data). For each DMC invariant it records what each host harness actually guarantees, so no invariant degrades silently and `dmc doctor` can render each host honestly.
faithful_to: docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md §3 (degraded-invariant matrix — the eight rows below are §3 verbatim in intent)
provenance.milestone: dmc-v1 M8 (P20 data file; M10 owns the narrative doc)
provenance.model_name_free: Harness IDENTIFIERS only (claude-code, codex, opencode). No model-version-name string appears here; those live solely in the dated orchestration/models.json. The harness ids do not match the narrow model-version detector.
provenance.honesty_rule: The Codex column NEVER claims an enforced-class guarantee: no cell in the `codex` field asserts runtime enforcement. `dmc doctor` renders this matrix PER-HOST (each physical line names exactly one harness) and reports the DMC mode host-independently, so the Codex-scoped honesty control stays fail-closed-sound.
provenance.dmc_mode_reporting: host-independent — the DMC mode (active | passive | off) is read from .harness/mode (absent => active) and reported on its own line, never attributed to a single harness.

classification_legend.enforced: a runtime host mechanism denies/blocks before the effect (claude-code column only)
classification_legend.advisory: wired but not a runtime boundary on this host; a named backstop closes the gap
classification_legend.stub: Ring-1 adapter not built for this host (absent)

invariants.title: Scope lock on edits
invariants.claude-code: enforced — PreToolUse Edit|Write deny before write
invariants.codex: advisory — PreToolUse over edit tools (deny before write); tool-input field names unproven at the M6.5 spike; backstop: the post-Bash diff guard

invariants.title: Scope lock on Bash writes
invariants.claude-code: enforced — Bash write-radius classify (L0 git-apply/patch deny always, L1 scope adjudication when armed) + post-Bash diff guard
invariants.codex: advisory — PreToolUse Bash is non-airtight (unified_exec and non-shell paths evade interception); PRIMARY safety net: the M6 post-Bash diff guard

invariants.title: Secret-path read deny
invariants.codex: advisory — PreToolUse path-only, pending a tool-input field-name shim; backstop: the instruction-level secret rule (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)

invariants.title: Stop / completion gate
invariants.codex: advisory — Stop decision:block parity, envelope honoring unproven at the M6.5 spike; backstop: the pre-commit/CI release gate

invariants.title: Natural activation (suffix routing)
invariants.claude-code: enforced — UserPromptSubmit router (Ring-0 owns the routing logic)
invariants.codex: advisory — UserPromptSubmit parity; both hosts delegate to the Ring-0 router (no material residual gap)

invariants.title: Approval gates
invariants.claude-code: enforced — ask prompts via settings.json + the human-only gate
invariants.codex: advisory — approval_policy + PermissionRequest hook; the human-only gate is Ring-0 and host-independent

invariants.title: Hook trust
invariants.claude-code: enforced — hooks active once wired in settings.json
invariants.codex: advisory — non-managed hooks need a one-time /hooks content-hash trust; a changed hook is skipped until re-trusted; the installer surfaces the trust step and never bypasses it

invariants.title: Protected DMC bindings
invariants.claude-code: scope-guard-dependent — .claude/** is editable by the agent unless a scope guard denies it
invariants.codex: stronger asymmetry — the OS sandbox keeps <root>/.codex, <root>/.agents and <root>/.git read-only to the agent even in workspace-write, so the agent cannot self-edit its own DMC bindings at runtime
